report_name: Ransomware Frenzy: INC, ANUBIS, Qilin and Bashe Hit Raft of Global Firms

threat_actors.name: INC Ransom
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that claimed responsibility for data breaches at multiple organizations in Brazil and the United States in early July 2026, using double-extortion tactics.

threat_actors.name: ANUBIS
threat_actors.description: A ransomware gang that targeted organizations in Switzerland and the United States in early July 2026, listing them on its data leak site.

threat_actors.name: Bashe
threat_actors.description: A threat actor that claimed responsibility for data breaches at a SaaS firm in Italy and a conglomerate in Vietnam in early July 2026.

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: A well-known Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) group that attacked a golf club in Australia as part of a global wave of attacks in early July 2026.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware groups encrypted victim systems as part of their double-extortion campaigns.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware groups stole data from victim networks before encryption, a key component of their double-extortion strategy.

malware.name: INC Ransomware
malware.description: Ransomware used by the INC Ransom group in their double-extortion attacks.

malware.name: Anubis
malware.description: Ransomware likely used by the ANUBIS gang in their extortion campaigns.

malware.name: Qilin
malware.description: Ransomware used by the Qilin RaaS group in their attack on an Australian entity.

identities.name: Carvalima Transportes
identities.description: A Brazilian logistics firm listed as a victim by the INC Ransom group.

identities.name: Estrutural Zortéa
identities.description: A Brazilian industrial engineering company listed as a victim by the INC Ransom group.

identities.name: Hamilton Eye Institute
identities.description: A US healthcare provider listed as a victim by the INC Ransom group.

identities.name: City of Acworth, Georgia
identities.description: A US municipal government listed as a victim by the INC Ransom group.

identities.name: Oak Park, Michigan
identities.description: A US municipal government listed as a victim by the INC Ransom group.

identities.name: Ferrum Group
identities.description: A Swiss industrial manufacturer listed as a victim by the ANUBIS gang.

identities.name: Quest Healthcare Solutions
identities.description: A US healthcare staffing agency listed as a victim by the ANUBIS gang.

identities.name: Northeast Pediatrics & Adolescent Medicine
identities.description: A US healthcare provider listed as a victim by the ANUBIS gang.

identities.name: Flazio
identities.description: An Italian SaaS website-building platform listed as a victim by the threat actor Bashe.

identities.name: Rita Võ Group
identities.description: A Vietnamese business conglomerate listed as a victim by the threat actor Bashe.

identities.name: Pennant Hills Golf Club
identities.description: An Australian private golf course listed as a victim by the Qilin group.

indicators.name: Ransomware TTP: VSS Deletion with WMIC
indicators.pattern:
title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via WMIC
id: 2b8b3235-1442-4399-825f-2300081335b4
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of wmic.exe to delete volume shadow copies. This is a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-04
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'shadowcopy'
      - 'delete'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of `wmic.exe shadowcopy delete`, a common ransomware technique to prevent system recovery, mentioned as a hunting hint in the report.

indicators.name: Ransomware TTP: Add User to Domain Admins
indicators.pattern:
title: Addition of User to Domain Admins Group
id: 9c582e19-31e1-4a5a-9e7f-3e5d78889a2b
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'net.exe' to add a user to the 'Domain Admins' group, a common privilege escalation and persistence technique.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-04
tags:
  - attack.t1098
  - attack.persistence
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'group'
      - 'Domain Admins'
      - '/add'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of the `net group "Domain Admins" /add` command, a hunting hint provided for detecting privilege escalation activity related to ransomware groups.

indicators.name: Rclone Execution For Data Exfiltration
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Rclone Execution Detected
id: f4d8a2c1-0b3e-4f9a-8d6c-1a2b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of rclone.exe, a tool often abused by ransomware groups for data exfiltration to cloud storage services.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-04
tags:
  - attack.t1537
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\rclone.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of `rclone.exe`, a tool mentioned as a hunting hint for data exfiltration activities by ransomware groups.
